typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Provider routing runtime path is not wired to the new scorecard ranking: route() still selects candidateRoutes(...).first and candidateRoutes() still returns legacy selectRoutes ordering, so capability/cost/latency/trust/policy-fit ranking is not used for production route/failover decisions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require mission-core-worker handoffs to always include the optional EndFeatureRun handoff.skillFeedback block (explicitly mark followedProcedure true/false) to improve scrutiny traceability.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m7-router-scoring-layer handoff file /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/handoffs/2026-04-19T21-20-34-985Z__m7-router-scoring-layer__1587cb2b-efa7-47db-816e-5474e1a337cb.json omitted handoff.skillFeedback, reducing procedural-audit context for review.

rejectedObservations.observation: Architecture docs in .factory/library/architecture.md describe precedence ordering while implementation uses weighted composite scoring.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous ownership/source-of-truth during a failing feature review; cannot confidently classify whether docs or implementation should be changed until the blocking runtime wiring issue is fixed.
